summary:
Nintedanib has no established effect on improving response rates in bladder cancer based on current clinical evidence. Available data do not support its use in this specific indication due to lack of trial results linking it to enhanced treatment outcomes in urothelial carcinoma.

The available information shows that nintedanib lacks demonstrated efficacy in improving response rates among patients with bladder cancer. No studies link nintedanib to better responses in urothelial carcinoma; standard treatments remain preferred without supportive evidence from targeted therapies like nintedanib.
